summary: This notebook provides a baseline training pipeline for 2D particle detection using the YOLO11 architecture on the CZII competition dataset. It leverages a pre-adapted YOLO-formatted dataset, loads a pretrained YOLO11-L checkpoint, and trains the model for 100 epochs with configured data augmentations and optimization hyperparameters. The author validates the trained model using standard mAP metrics and demonstrates a quick inference step on a validation image. Readers should learn how to rapidly deploy a YOLO-based object detection baseline, understand the impact of specific augmentations like mixup and copy-paste, and see how Ultralytics simplifies the training workflow.
competition_flow: Loads a YOLO-formatted dataset via YAML, trains a pretrained YOLO11-L model for 100 epochs with data augmentations and AdamW optimizer, validates it using mAP metrics, and demonstrates inference on a sample image.
data_reading: Reads dataset paths and annotations from a YAML configuration file (czii_conf.yaml) that structures the data for YOLO object detection format.
data_processing: Applies real-time training augmentations including vertical/horizontal flips (0.5 probability), rotation (±45°), shear (5°), mixup (0.2), and copy-paste (0.25).
insights: Strong augmentations like mixup and copy-paste are effective for improving detection performance on particle imagery.
Using a pretrained YOLO11 model with AdamW and cosine learning rate scheduling provides a stable and efficient training baseline.
The Ultralytics YAML configuration format allows quick adaptation of custom datasets without modifying core training code.
